digest.component_or_area: MCP tool reference generator + CI drift gate
propositions: Generate docs/mcp-reference.md from the MCP server's own registrars and enforce it with a --check drift gate in CI, mirroring the gen_env_registry.py pattern.

The generator must enumerate each registrar with REBAR_MCP_READONLY=0 so write tools register, since only REBAR_MCP_READONLY changes list_tools membership and a naive single call yields a partial reference.

Tools are classified by which registrar registers them plus a closed four-entry override table (reconcile, fsck, sign_review, run_workflow), emitted into three labeled gate-tier sections with the special cases as inline annotations.
The reference must list all 47 tools (23 read + 6 llm + 18 write) with sign_review placed under Write-gated (not LLM-gated) and reconcile annotated as jira-sync and readonly gated.
A held-out unit test uses section-bounded slicing as an independent oracle to prove special-case placement rather than re-running the generator's own classifier.
The drift gate must be wired into both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml, with a parity test asserting every scripts/*.py in test.yml also appears in gerrit-verify.yaml.
